title: The Elements of Style
author: Strunk & White
content: Vigorous writing is concise. A sentence should contain no unnecessary words, a paragraph no unnecessary sentences, for the same reason that a drawing should have no unnecessary lines and a machine no unnecessary parts. This requires not that the writer make all his sentences short, or that he avoid all detail and treat his subjects only in outline, but that every word tell. One cannot think well with cramped language. Clear thinking becomes clear writing; one can't exist without the other. The mind of the writer must be clear before he can write clearly. The writer must think clearly before he can express himself clearly. If one's thinking is muddy, one's writing will be muddy too.
questions_raised: How does clear thinking relate to ethical decision-making?
Can concise communication help prevent misunderstandings that lead to harm?
Is there such a thing as 'unnecessary' truth in communication?
insights_gained: Clear thinking is essential for ethical action
Communication should serve understanding, not confusion
Efficiency in thought leads to efficiency in helping others
